context:
Tsar Putin

Recension av Anna Arutunyan: Tsar Putin, Ordfront, 2012.

NГӨr Vladimir Putin i mitten av juli kom till Krymsk, en by uppslukad av lera, var det ett tecken pГҘ nГҘd. NГӨr poliВӯ tiken ГӨr lika gyttjig och intransparent som leran, som krГӨvt minst 171 dГ¶dsoffer, stГҘr det enda hoppet till ett ingriВӯ pande frГҘn ovan. Bara den hГ¶gsta makten kan man tro pГҘ. Putins blotta nГӨrvaro blir tecknet pГҘ att nГҘgon dГӨr uppe bryr sig, och den oundvikliga upplГӨxningen av lokala tjГӨnstemГӨn beviset fГ¶r att man inte ГӨr Г¶vergiven i en vГӨrld dГӨr folket och suverГӨnen ГӨr ohjГӨlpligt sammanbundna.

Г–versvГӨmningarna i Krymsk och Putins visit intrГӨffar efter det att Anna Arutunyan, journalist fГ¶dd i Ryssland men uppvГӨxt i USA, skrivit sin bok, men sГҘdant ГӨr staВӯ dens Г¶de ur hennes synvinkel. Trots titeln handlar boken inte om Putin. Putins stГҘlgrГҘ blick, ofta mytologiserad, ser hon som ett uttryck fГ¶r hans kameleontlika natur. Putin ГӨr egentligen ointressant. Han ГӨr den han fГ¶rvГӨntas vara, och som Rysslands ledare ГҘterspeglar Putin, enligt Arutunyan, Ryssland som det fГ¶rvГӨntas vara.

Beskrivningen av Putin som en naturlig konsekvens av det ryska samhГӨllet, ett tomt kГӨrl i vilket man lГӨgger sina fГ¶rutfattade meningar, har kallats fjГӨskande. Det ГӨr orГӨttВӯ vist. Men det ГӨr nГҘgot beklГӨmmande i att en bok som fГ¶rst ifrГҘgasГӨtter den masochistiska fГ¶restГӨllningen om att ryssar inte ГӨr lГӨmpade fГ¶r demokrati ГӨndГҘ beskriver en вҖқfolksjГӨlвҖқ som tyr sig till ett korrupt och maktfullkomligt system.

Om man bortser frГҘn den nГӨstan deterministiska beskrivningen av underkastelse i Ryssland skГӨnker boken

prompt: Vilket av fГ¶ljande skilde inte intervenВӯ tionsgruppen frГҘn kontrollgruppen, enligt texten?

options.text: Andelen patienter som tre dagar efter operation hade gasavgГҘng.
Andelen patienter med diagnoserna kolontumГ¶r respektive divertikulit.

context:
NГӨringsdryck infГ¶r tarmoperation

StГ¶rre kirurgiska ingrepp innebГӨr stora pГҘfrestningar fГ¶r individen. Vid alla typer av kroppsskada sker snabba fГ¶rВӯ ГӨndringar av ГӨmnesomsГӨttningen. Detta innebГӨr en negaВӯ tiv belastning fГ¶r kroppen samtidigt som frisГӨttningen av bland annat stresshormoner Г¶kar. Patienter med allvarВӯ liga symtom frГҘn magвҖ“tarm-kanalen har oftast lГҘngt fГ¶re operationstillfГӨllet svГҘrigheter med en fullvГӨrdig nutrition, vilket bidrar till ett ytterligare fГ¶rsГӨmrat allmГӨntillstГҘnd infГ¶r operationen. Dessutom fГ¶rbereds patienter som ska genomgГҘ kolonkirurgi traditionsenligt med dietfГ¶re- skrifter, laxering och fasta.

Under 1990-talet aktualiserades problemet allt mer. Eftersom vi arbetar med denna patientgrupp bestГӨmde vi oss fГ¶r att ta reda pГҘ om kolhydratuppladdning med nГӨringsdryck en vecka fГ¶re kolonkirurgi har nГҘgon positiv effekt pГҘ patienternas postoperativa tillfrisknande.

Studien kom att omfatta totalt 55 patienter i tvГҘ grupВӯ per: en interventionsgrupp med 30 personer och en konВӯ trollgrupp med 25 personer. Patienterna var i ГҘldrarna 20 till 80 ГҘr och hade nГҘgon av diagnoserna kolontumГ¶r eller divertikulit. Under perioden april 1999 till maj 2001 genomgick de planerad kolonkirurgi sГҘsom hemikolekВӯ tomi, sigmoideumresektion och anteriorresektion. I interВӯ ventionsgruppen ingick 21 kvinnor och 9 mГӨn, medan kontrollgruppen bestod av 15 kvinnor och 10 mГӨn. Medel- ГҘldern var drygt 61 ГҘr i bГҘda grupperna. FГ¶rdelningen av diagnoser var densamma i bГҘda grupperna.

I interventionsgruppen fick samtliga patienter fГ¶re opeВӯ rationen ett kolhydrattillskott i form av 200 milliliter av en nГӨringsdryck som gav 125 kilokalorier per 100 milliliter. Tillskottet gavs morgon och kvГӨll under sju dagar fГ¶re planerad operation.

Kontrollgruppen bestod av patienter som hade samma diagnoser som interventionsgruppen och som ГҘret fГ¶re genomgГҘtt en kolonoperation. OperationsfГ¶rberedelserna var desamma som i interventionsgruppen, med undantag fГ¶r kolhydrattillskottet.

Patienterna som ingick i interventionsgruppen tillВӯ frГҘgades muntligt och fick skriftlig information. Vid mottagningsbesГ¶ket fick de flesta patienterna med sig nГӨringsdrycken hem, medan andra valde att hГӨmta den pГҘ vГҘrdavdelningen. NГӨringsdrycken var kostnadsfri.

Tidpunkten fГ¶r postoperativ gasavgГҘng och post- operativ faecesavgГҘng samt vГҘrdtidens lГӨngd studerades. Г–nskvГӨrt hade varit att mГӨta serumalbuminvГӨrdet, dГҘ det har betydelse fГ¶r bland annat sГҘrlГӨkningen. Det var inte genomfГ¶rbart dГҘ vГӨrdet saknades i mГҘnga av kontrollВӯ gruppens journaler.

Eftersom det ГӨr viktigt fГ¶r patientens vГӨlbefinnande att tarmen fungerar, var valet av dessa kriterier betydelsefullt ur ett omvГҘrdnadsperspektiv. Tidpunkten fГ¶r nГӨr tarmen kommer i gГҘng ГӨr ГӨven avgГ¶rande fГ¶r vГҘrdtidens lГӨngd.

Vad gГӨller tarmfunktionen kunde i vГҘr studie inga negativa komplikationer pГҘvisas med att ge patienten ett kolhydrattillskott. Patienterna i interventionsgruppen hade tidigare gas- och faecesavgГҘng ГӨn kontrollgruppen. HГӨlften av samtliga patienter i interventionsgruppen hade gasavgГҘng inom tre dagar efter operation jГӨmfГ¶rt med 40 procent i kontrollgruppen. Medelantalet dagar frГҘn operaВӯ tion till gasavgГҘng var 3,5 i interventionsgruppen jГӨmfГ¶rt med 4,0 i kontrollgruppen.

Tidpunkten fГ¶r den fГ¶rsta avfГ¶ringen efter operation var i medeltal 5,0 dagar i interventionsgruppen jГӨmfГ¶rt med 5,3 dagar i kontrollgruppen. I interventionsgruppen hade 70 procent avfГ¶ring inom de fem fГ¶rsta dagarna efter operation jГӨmfГ¶rt med 56 procent i kontrollgruppen.

I interventionsgruppen gick 50 procent av patienterna hem inom sju dagar jГӨmfГ¶rt med 32 procent i kontrollВӯ gruppen. Antalet vГҘrddagar varierade mellan 3 och 16 dagar i de bГҘda grupperna, median 7,5 i interventionsВӯ gruppen (5 till 13 dagar) och median 8,0 (3 till 16 dagar) i kontrollgruppen. Generellt kan sГӨgas att vГҘrdtiderna blir allt kortare fГ¶r varje ГҘr, vilket kan fГ¶rklara skillnaden.

AnestesifГ¶reskrifterna har ГӨndrats sedan studien gjorВӯ des. Patienterna behГ¶ver inte lГӨngre fasta frГҘn midnatt, utan tillГҘts dricka fram till tre timmar fГ¶re anestesi. Nu skulle det alltsГҘ vara mГ¶jligt att lГҘta patienten inta den sista drycken sГҘ sent som pГҘ operationsdagens morgon, vilket kanske ytterligare skulle kunna fГ¶rbГӨttra patientens allmГӨnstatus.

MГҘnga patienter i studien upplevde det positivt att dricka nГӨringsdrycken. De uttryckte ett vГӨlbefinnande och att de kГӨnde sig i gott skick infГ¶r operationen. VГӨlbefinВӯ nande ГӨr svГҘrt att mГӨta, dГҘ det ГӨr en subjektiv upplevelse. Men vГҘr uppfattning ГӨr ГӨndГҘ, utifrГҘn vГҘr kliniska erfaВӯ renhet, att patienternas vГӨlbefinnande Г¶kade genom kol- hydrattillskottet. Patienter som har ГҘterkommit fГ¶r andra operationer har Г¶nskat nГӨringsdryck ГӨven dГҘ.

NutritionsfrГҘgor ГӨr en viktig del i vГҘrden. Vi tycker att de bГ¶r uppmГӨrksammas ytterligare genom att det uppВӯ rГӨttas sГҘ kallade nГӨringsrГҘd som arbetar fГ¶r att lyfta fram nutritionsproblem. Г„ven tidigare rutiner infГ¶r operationer bГ¶r ses Г¶ver, dГҘ anestesins fГ¶reskrifter har ГӨndrats marВӯ kant. En kommande studie skulle kunna innefatta stГ¶rre patientgrupper och intag av nГӨringsdryck ГӨven pГҘ operaВӯ tionsdagens morgon. Man skulle ocksГҘ kunna undersГ¶ka

om och i vilken grad serumalbuminet pГҘverkas av ett extra kolhydrattillskott, eftersom hypoalbuminemi har visat sig kunna orsaka lГҘngsammare sГҘrlГӨkning. I en randomiserad, kontrollerad studie skulle patientens allmГӨntillstГҘnd och vГӨlbefinnande kunna fГ¶ljas en lГӨngre tid.

Lena Sahlin-Г–sterlund, Inger Forsman,

Gunhild Dahlberg & Ingrid Lindqvist

Not all Africans in India were imported as slaves.

context:
Africans in the Indian Muti

Throughout 2007, the 200th anniversary of the abolition of slavery, the focus was naturally on the shipment of Afri- can slaves to the West Indies, but no mention was made of the trade eastwards from Africa to the Indian subcontinent or of the Arab traders who sold slaves to wealthy Indian rulers. Although a few Africans rose to positions of power in India, some even establishing small kingdoms, most entered the country as slaves. Their descendants are found today in Gujarat and along the southern coast. But little is known about those slaves shipped in through Bombay and Karachi and taken eastwards to be sold. Anecdotal stories of African soldiers fighting against the British during the Indian Mutiny (also known as the Indian Uprising) occur in military memoirs and par- ticularly those from Oudh, the last princely state to be annexed by the East India Company. The two events, the annexation in 1856 followed by the mutiny of 1857вҖ“58, are closely connected. Many of the Indian troops who turned against their British officers came from Oudh. Although there was much wrong in the kingdom, presided over by its last monarch the Nawab Wajid Ali Shah (1822вҖ“87), the inhabitants preferred to be ruled by their own king, whatever his faults, rather than by the British. This was something that Lord Dalhousie, the Governor General of India, had signally failed to appreciate when he ordered Oudh to be annexed on the grounds of misrule. Some of the fiercest Mutiny fighting took place in its capital, Lucknow, where the Nawab had recently created a huge palace. During the siege and the subsequent recap- ture of the city in March 1858, African men and women were involved in the thick of the street fighting. Why these men and women supported their former master, the slave- owning Wajid Ali Shah, rather than the British, who had prohibited slavery, can be answered from British Govern- ment records in the Delhi National Archives. There are fragmentary accounts of Africans in Lucknow brought in by earlier rulers. Wajid Ali ShahвҖҷs great-grand- father, a keen horseman, had employed вҖҳhabshi boysвҖҷ as jockeys at the turn of the 19th century. A great-uncle had iny ordered and bought 18 slaves, who arrived at Bombay in an Arab ship and who were brought overland in covered carriages. Wajid Ali ShahвҖҷs own father ordered 200 swords for his African troops. So the custom of employing Afri- cans and an overland slave-trading route was already estab- lished. As many as 1,000 male and female African slaves may have arrived in Lucknow in 1847 and 1848. Although Africans were employed elsewhere in India, the Nawabs of Oudh seem unique in importing women as well as men. In 1860, with the British firmly back in control, the remaining Africans in Lucknow petitioned the Chief Commissioner for pensions. prompt: What is the main focus of the first two paragraphs?

options.text: The scientific basis of forensics has been greatly extended in recent years.
Those practising forensic science do not seem to realize its limitations.
Forensic evidence is often played down in criminal investigations.
The public are getting a false picture of what forensics is really like.

context:
Forensic Evidence

Thanks to TV programmes like CSI: Crime Scene Investi- gation, forensics is perceived as a glamorous science. In re- ality, of course, it is anything but, and in some cases it isnвҖҷt particularly scientific either. Fingerprints, tool marks and similar evidence can be invaluable in bringing a criminal to justice. The problem is that expert witnesses have tended to exaggerate what forensic techniques can tell us. Forensic science has to transform itself. For a role model it could look to DNA analysis, with its careful attention to what the evidence can and cannot tell us. There are signs a change is already under way, but it is not happening fast enough. The big obstacle is the courts. Expert witnesses command great authority in the eyes of jurors and judges, and problems arise when the so-called experts claim greater precision for their techniques than they can support. In the US, some courts have adopted screening mecha- nisms to try to prevent untrustworthy experts from testi- fying, but this is a problem when judges without exten- sive scientific training have to decide whether practitioners know what they are talking about. Courts have tradi- tionally deferred to вҖңthe scientific communityвҖқ to help them distinguish respected practitioners from charlatans. However, in some forensic sciences, many of the practi- tioners are not scientists. This was the difficulty faced by the Supreme Judicial Court of Massachusetts in a recent murder trial. The point at issue was the reliability of fingerprint identification. The state argued that, since all fingerprint examiners believe fingerprint examination is fail-safe, the court should back it. The defendant argued that a broad cross-section of sci- entists and scholars who had studied the issue agreed that the reliability of fingerprinting will never be known until its accuracy is measured вҖ“ something that has yet to be done. The court backed the practitioners. It ruled that, in order to be admissible evidence, fingerprinting needed to be accepted only among practising fingerprint examiners. This was a victory for the state, and a defeat for science. According to the courts logic, any community of practi- tioners is now entitled to assess its own reliability. This is unfortunate because many forensic disciplines appear ill equipped or disinclined to take a rigorous empi- rical approach. A century of fingerprint practice went by before the first attempt to measure its accuracy was published. The FBI recently laid out an ambitious research agenda to place fingerprinting on a more scientific foot- ing, but only after it was forced to admit that a false finger- print identification had wrongly implicated an Oregon lawyer in the Madrid train bombings of 2004. A good illustration of the lack of research underpinning much forensic science is the use by the FBI of an esoteric technique called comparative bullet lead analysis (CBLA), which rested on untested assumptions about the diversity of lead sources and their chemical consistency. The validity of these assumptions was only addressed in the 1990s. In 2004, the FBI discontinued CBLA after a critical report from the National Research Council (NRC). What does this say about the state of forensic science? At a National Academy of Sciences colloquium, Ronald Singer, former president of the American Academy of Forensic Sciences, said the CBLA episode showed forensic science was вҖңself-correctingвҖқ. That is a generous interpreta- tion, considering that it took more than 30 years for the FBI to fund the NRC report. HereвҖҷs another way of viewing it: the FBIвҖҷs use of CBLA turned the usual relationship between law and science on its head. Rather than performing scientific research to determine what can responsibly be said about a form of evidence, and then offering such testimony in court, it appears the FBI testified first and did the science later. Change is coming. Scientists from many disciplines are taking an interest in forensic science. But unless the courts stop rewarding practitioners for ignoring science, the revo- lution is likely to take longer than it should.

Simon A. Cole, New Scientist
